title: CLAUDE.md
text: taxdiv calculates taxonomic diversity indices ecological communities using three approaches: Classical indices (classical_indices.R): Shannon, Simpson — species-level diversity Clarke & Warwick (classical_indices.R): Delta, Delta*, AvTD, VarTD — taxonomy-aware distinctness Ozkan pTO (ozkan_pto.R): Deng entropy-based proportional taxonomic originality — package’s core contribution Ozkan pTO pipeline three sequential runs: - Run 1 (ozkan_pto.R): Deterministic calculation 8 indices (, +, uTO, pTO, etc.) - Run 2 (ozkan_pto_resample.R): Stochastic bootstrap resampling confidence intervals - Run 3 (ozkan_pto_sensitivity.R): Jackknife sensitivity analysis (species removal impact) ozkan_pto_full() chains three runs together.

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: taxdiv paketi, ekolojik topluluklarda taksonomik cesitliligi hesaplamak icin yazilmis bir R paketidir. Klasik cesitlilik indeksleri (Shannon, Simpson) sadece tur bolluklarini dikkate alir. Ancak iki toplulukta ayni sayida tur olsa bile, birinde tum turler ayni familyadan, digerinde farkli takimlardan olabilir. Taksonomik cesitlilik indeksleri bu farki yakalayabilir. Pakette uc ana yaklasim vardir: Klasik indeksler: Shannon ve Simpson Clarke & Warwick taksonomik ayirt edicilik: Delta, Delta*, AvTD, VarTD Ozkan (2018) Deng entropisi tabanli: pTO (uTO, , uTO+, +)
code: library(taxdiv)

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Shannon ve Simpson indeksleri sadece tur bolluklarini kullanir. Taksonomik yapiyi dikkate almazlar. Yorum: Shannon degeri ne kadar yuksekse topluluk o kadar cesitlidir. Simpson baskinlik degeri ne kadar dusukse o kadar iyi dagilim vardir. Shannon ve Simpson icin correction = "chao_shen" parametresiyle Chao & Shen (2003) yanliliksiz tahminci kullanilabilir:
code:
# Shannon cesitliligi (dogal logaritma) H <- shannon(topluluk) cat("Shannon H':", round(H, 4), "
") #> Shannon H': 2.1692  # Simpson indeksleri D <- simpson(topluluk, type = "dominance")       # Baskinlik GS <- simpson(topluluk, type = "gini_simpson")    # 1 - D inv_D <- simpson(topluluk, type = "inverse")      # 1 / D  cat("Simpson baskinlik (D):", round(D, 4), "
") #> Simpson baskinlik (D): 0.1269 cat("Gini-Simpson (1-D):", round(GS, 4), "
") #> Gini-Simpson (1-D): 0.8731 cat("Ters Simpson (1/D):", round(inv_D, 4), "
") #> Ters Simpson (1/D): 7.8782 H_duz <- shannon(topluluk, correction = "chao_shen") cat("Shannon (Chao-Shen duzeltmeli):", round(H_duz, 4), "
") #> Shannon (Chao-Shen duzeltmeli): 2.17 cat("Shannon (duzeltmesiz):         ", round(H, 4), "
") #> Shannon (duzeltmesiz):          2.1692

text: Taksonomik cesitliligi hesaplamadan turler arasi taksonomik mesafeyi hesaplamamiz gerekir. Bu mesafe, iki turun taksonomik hiyerarsideki ilk ortak atalarinin seviyesine gore belirlenir. Nasil okunur: 0 = Ayni tur (kosegen) 1 = Ayni cins, farkli tur (ornegin Pinus brutia - Pinus nigra) 2 = Ayni familya, farkli cins (ornegin Pinus - Cedrus, ikisi de Pinaceae) 3 = Ayni takim, farkli familya (ornegin Pinaceae - Cupressaceae, ikisi de Pinales) 6 = Farkli sinif (Magnoliopsida vs Pinopsida — en uzak mesafe)

what: Adim 3: Clarke & Warwick Taksonomik Ayirt Edicilik
title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Clarke & Warwick (1998) dort farkli olcu onerir: Onemli fark: Delta ve Delta*: Bolluga bagimli — tur sayilari onemli AvTD ve VarTD: Bolluktan bagimsiz — sadece turlerin var/yok bilgisini kullanir
code:
# Delta: Taksonomik cesitlilik (bolluk agirlikli) d <- delta(topluluk, agac) cat("Delta (taksonomik cesitlilik):", round(d, 4), "
") #> Delta (taksonomik cesitlilik): 3.8246  # Delta*: Taksonomik ayirt edicilik (bolluk agirlikli, ayni tur ciftleri haric) ds <- delta_star(topluluk, agac) cat("Delta* (taksonomik ayirt edicilik):", round(ds, 4), "
") #> Delta* (taksonomik ayirt edicilik): 4.3515  # AvTD (Delta+): Ortalama taksonomik ayirt edicilik (sadece tur listesi, bolluk onemsiz) turler <- names(topluluk) avg_td <- avtd(turler, agac) cat("AvTD (Delta+):", round(avg_td, 4), "
") #> AvTD (Delta+): 4.3111  # VarTD (Lambda+): Taksonomik ayirt edicilik varyasyonu var_td <- vartd(turler, agac) cat("VarTD (Lambda+):", round(var_td, 4), "
") #> VarTD (Lambda+): 3.5032

what: Adim 4: Deng Entropisi ve Ozkan pTO (Islem 1)
title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Deng (2016) entropisi, Shannon entropisinin Dempster-Shafer kanit teorisi uzerinden genellenmis halidir. taksonomik seviyede gruplarin tur sayisini (fokal eleman boyutu) dikkate alir. Ozkan (2018) Deng entropisini kullanarak dort olcu tanimlar: uTO: Agirliksiz taksonomik cesitlilik : Agirlikli taksonomik cesitlilik (seviye agirliklari ile) uTO+: Agirliksiz taksonomik uzaklik (sadece tur listesi) +: Agirlikli taksonomik uzaklik taksonomik seviyedeki Deng entropisi degerleri:
code:
sonuc <- ozkan_pto(topluluk, agac)  cat("uTO  (agirliksiz cesitlilik):", round(sonuc$uTO, 4), "
") #> uTO  (agirliksiz cesitlilik): 8.6804 cat("TO   (agirlikli cesitlilik):", round(sonuc$TO, 4), "
") #> TO   (agirlikli cesitlilik): 15.2596 cat("uTO+ (agirliksiz uzaklik):", round(sonuc$uTO_plus, 4), "
") #> uTO+ (agirliksiz uzaklik): 8.9019 cat("TO+  (agirlikli uzaklik):", round(sonuc$TO_plus, 4), "
") #> TO+  (agirlikli uzaklik): 15.4812 cat("Taksonomik seviye bazinda Deng entropisi:
") #> Taksonomik seviye bazinda Deng entropisi: for (i in seq_along(sonuc$Ed_levels)) {   cat("  ", names(sonuc$Ed_levels)[i], ":",       round(sonuc$Ed_levels[i], 4), "
") } #>    Species : 2.3026  #>    Genus : 2.5459  #>    Family : 3.1666  #>    Order : 4.2421  #>    Class : 4.2421  #>    Phylum : 4.2421  #>    Kingdom : 0

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: ozkan_pto() fonksiyonunda max_level parametresi ile hesaplamada kullanilacak maksimum taksonomik seviye sinirlanabilir. Varsayilan NULL tum seviyeleri kullanir. "auto" bilgi icermeyen seviyeleri otomatik eler:
code:
# Tum seviyeler (varsayilan) r_full <- ozkan_pto(topluluk, agac, max_level = NULL) cat("Tum seviyeler: uTO =", round(r_full$uTO, 4),     " TO =", round(r_full$TO, 4), "
") #> Tum seviyeler: uTO = 8.6804  TO = 15.2596  # Otomatik seviye secimi r_auto <- ozkan_pto(topluluk, agac, max_level = "auto") cat("Auto seviye:   uTO =", round(r_auto$uTO, 4),     " TO =", round(r_auto$TO, 4), "
") #> Auto seviye:   uTO = 8.6804  TO = 15.2596 cat("Bilgilendirici seviye:", r_auto$max_informative_level, "
") #> Bilgilendirici seviye: 5  # Sadece ilk 3 seviye r_3 <- ozkan_pto(topluluk, agac, max_level = 3) cat("Ilk 3 seviye:  uTO =", round(r_3$uTO, 4),     " TO =", round(r_3$TO, 4), "
") #> Ilk 3 seviye:  uTO = 8.6804  TO = 15.2596

text: max_level kullanildiginda ozkan_pto() hem standart hem max_level degerlerini dondurur: pto_components() ile tek satirda alabilirsiniz:
code:
sonuc8 <- ozkan_pto(topluluk, agac, max_level = "auto") cat("Standart:  uTO =", round(sonuc8$uTO, 4),     " TO =", round(sonuc8$TO, 4), "
") #> Standart:  uTO = 8.6804  TO = 15.2596 cat("Max-level: uTO_max =", round(sonuc8$uTO_max, 4),     " TO_max =", round(sonuc8$TO_max, 4), "
") #> Max-level: uTO_max = 8.6804  TO_max = 15.2596 cat("Bilgilendirici seviye sayisi:", sonuc8$max_informative_level, "
") #> Bilgilendirici seviye sayisi: 5 pto_components(topluluk, agac) #>          uTO           TO     uTO_plus      TO_plus      uTO_max       TO_max  #>     8.680386    15.259632     8.901929    15.481180     8.680386    15.259632  #> uTO_plus_max  TO_plus_max  #>     8.901929    15.481180

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Islem 1 tum turleri oldugu gibi kullanir. Peki topluluk yapisini degistirirsek cesitlilik nasil etkilenir? Islem 2 bunu test eder: turde %50 olasilikla dahil/haric birakarak rastgele alt topluluklar olusturur. Birden fazla iterasyon yapilir ve maksimum pTO degeri alinir. Islem 2 degerleri zaman Islem 1’e esit veya buyuktur, cunku deterministik hesaplama ilk iterasyon olarak dahil edilir.
code:
islem2 <- ozkan_pto_resample(topluluk, agac, n_iter = 101, seed = 42)  cat("=== Islem 1 (deterministik) ===
") #> === Islem 1 (deterministik) === cat("uTO+:", round(islem2$uTO_plus_det, 4), "
") #> uTO+: 8.9019 cat("TO+: ", round(islem2$TO_plus_det, 4), "
") #> TO+:  15.4812 cat("uTO: ", round(islem2$uTO_det, 4), "
") #> uTO:  8.6804 cat("TO:  ", round(islem2$TO_det, 4), "

") #> TO:   15.2596  cat("=== Islem 2 (maksimum,", islem2$n_iter, "iterasyon) ===
") #> === Islem 2 (maksimum, 101 iterasyon) === cat("uTO+:", round(islem2$uTO_plus_max, 4), "
") #> uTO+: 8.9019 cat("TO+: ", round(islem2$TO_plus_max, 4), "
") #> TO+:  15.4812 cat("uTO: ", round(islem2$uTO_max, 4), "
") #> uTO:  8.6804 cat("TO:  ", round(islem2$TO_max, 4), "
") #> TO:   15.2596

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Islem 3, Islem 2 sonuclarina gore tur bazli dahil etme olasiliklari hesaplar. Cesitlilige daha cok katki saglayan turler farkli olasilikla dahil edilir.

") #> TO+:  15.9905 cat("uTO: ", round(islem3$uTO_max, 4), "

") #> TO:   15.5839

tam <- ozkan_pto_full(topluluk, agac, n_iter = 101, seed = 42)  cat("Pipeline: Islem 1 -> Islem 2 -> Islem 3

") #> Pipeline: Islem 1 -> Islem 2 -> Islem 3 cat("          uTO+      TO+       uTO       TO
") #>           uTO+      TO+       uTO       TO cat("Islem 1:", sprintf("%9.4f %9.4f %9.4f %9.4f",     tam$run1$uTO_plus, tam$run1$TO_plus,     tam$run1$uTO, tam$run1$TO), "
") #> Islem 1:    8.9019   15.4812    8.6804   15.2596 cat("Islem 2:", sprintf("%9.4f %9.4f %9.4f %9.4f",     tam$run2$uTO_plus_max, tam$run2$TO_plus_max,     tam$run2$uTO_max, tam$run2$TO_max), "
") #> Islem 2:    8.9019   15.4812    8.6804   15.2596 cat("Islem 3:", sprintf("%9.4f %9.4f %9.4f %9.4f",     tam$run3$uTO_plus_max, tam$run3$TO_plus_max,     tam$run3$uTO_max, tam$run3$TO_max), "
") #> Islem 3:    9.3926   15.9718    9.0047   15.5839

text: ozkan_pto_jackknife() birer birer tur cikararak (leave-one-) yanliliksiz pTO tahmini hesaplar:
code:
jk <- ozkan_pto_jackknife(topluluk, agac)  cat("Tam sonuc uTO+:", round(jk$full_result$uTO_plus, 4), "

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Bu bolum, paketteki indekslerin matematiksel formullerini ve birbirleriyle iliskilerini aciklar. Tez veya makale yazarken bu formullere ihtiyaciniz olacaktir.

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Shannon (1948), bir sistemdeki belirsizligi olcmek icin su formulu onermistir: H′=−∑=1Spiln(pi)H' = -\sum_{=1}^{S} p_i \ln(p_i) SS = toplam tur sayisi pip_i = ii. turun oransal bollugu (ni/Nn_i / N) NN = toplam birey sayisi Shannon entropisi turu bireysel bir olay olarak ele alir. Turler arasindaki akrabalik iliskilerini gormez.

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Simpson (1949), rastgele secilen iki bireyin ayni turden olma olasiligini olcer: D=∑=1Spi2D = \sum_{=1}^{S} p_i^2 Genellikle Gini-Simpson formu kullanilir: 1−D=1−∑=1Spi21 - D = 1 - \sum_{=1}^{S} p_i^2 Simpson, baskin turlere daha duyarlidir. Shannon ise nadir turlere daha fazla agirlik verir.

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Clarke & Warwick (1998) taksonomik hiyerarsiyi hesaba katan dort indeks tanimlamistir: Delta (Taksonomik cesitlilik, bolluk agirlikli): Δ=∑∑<jωij⋅ni⋅njN(N−1)/2\Delta = \frac{\sum \sum_{<j} \omega_{ij} \cdot n_i \cdot n_j} {N(N-1)/2} Delta+ (AvTD, sadece tur listesi): Δ+=∑∑<jωijS(S−1)/2\Delta^+ = \frac{\sum \sum_{<j} \omega_{ij}}{S(S-1)/2} Lambda+ (VarTD, varyasyon): Λ+=∑∑<j(ωij−Δ+)2S(S−1)/2\Lambda^+ = \frac{\sum \sum_{<j} (\omega_{ij} - \Delta^+)^2}{S(S-1)/2} Burada ωij\omega_{ij}, ii ve jj turleri arasindaki taksonomik mesafedir. Bu mesafe, iki turun taksonomik agacta ilk birlestigi seviyeye esittir (Cins = 1, Familya = 2, …, Alem = 6). Onemli: Δ+\Delta^+ ve Λ+\Lambda^+ bolluk bilgisi kullanmaz. Bu sayede farkli orneklem buyuklugundeki alanlari dogrudan karsilastirabilirsiniz (Clarke & Warwick, 1998, 2001).

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Deng (2016), Shannon entropisini Dempster-Shafer kanit teorisi (Dempster, 1967; Shafer, 1976) cercevesinde genellestirmistir. Temel fark: Shannon’da olay (tur) atomiktir. Deng entropisinde olaylar kume yapisi icerebilir. Bir taksonomik seviyedeki Deng entropisi: Ed=−∑=1Km(Fi)⋅lnm(Fi)2|Fi|−1E_d = -\sum_{=1}^{K} m(F_i) \cdot \ln\frac{m(F_i)}{2^{|F_i|} - 1} KK = o seviyedeki grup sayisi (ornegin familya sayisi) m(Fi)m(F_i) = ii. gruptaki turlerin oransal agirligi |Fi||F_i| = ii. gruptaki tur sayisi (fokal eleman boyutu) Shannon ile karsilastirma: Shannon entropisinde |Fi|=1|F_i| = 1 kabul edilir (tur tek basina bir olay). Bu durumda 2|Fi|−1=12^{|F_i|} - 1 = 1 olur ve Deng entropisi Shannon’indirgenir: Ed=−∑=1Km(Fi)⋅ln(m(Fi))=H′E_d = -\sum_{=1}^{K} m(F_i) \cdot \ln(m(F_i)) = H' Yani Deng entropisi Shannon’genellestirilmis halidir. Eger taksonomik gruplar 1’er turden olusuyorsa ikisi ayni sonucu verir.

title: taxdiv Paketi Kullanim Rehberi (Turkce)
text: Ozkan (2018), Deng entropisini kullanarak dort bilesenden olusan bir taksonomik cesitlilik olcusu tanimlar. Temel carpim (bir dilim icin): ∏=1L(wi⋅((eEdS)2eEdi+1))\prod_{=1}^{L} \left( w_i \cdot \left( \frac{(e^{E_d^S})^2}{e^{E_d^}} + 1 \right) \right) LL = aktif taksonomik seviye sayisi EdSE_d^S = tur seviyesindeki Deng entropisi EdiE_d^= ii. seviyedeki Deng entropisi wiw_i = seviye agirligi (agirliksiz icin wi=1w_i = 1, agirlikli icin wi=iw_i = ) pTO+ (Taksonomik mesafe, sadece Islem 1): pTO+=ln∏=1L(wi⋅((eEdS)2eEdi+1))pT_O^+ = \ln \prod_{=1}^{L} \left( w_i \cdot \left( \frac{(e^{E_d^S})^2}{e^{E_d^}} + 1 \right) \right) Sadece nk = 0 dilimini (tum turler dahil) kullanir. Bolluk bilgisine ihtiyac duymaz cunku tur listesiyle calisir. pTO (Taksonomik cesitlilik, Islem 1 + 2 + 3): pTO=ln(∑k=0ns(ns−nk)∏=1L(wi⋅((eEdS)2eEdi+1))ns+∑nk)pT_O = \ln \left( \frac{\sum_{k=0}^{n_s} (n_s - n_k) \prod_{=1}^{L} \left( w_i \cdot \left( \frac{(e^{E_d^S})^2} {e^{E_d^}} + 1 \right) \right)}{n_s + \sum n_k} \right) Dilim proseduru (slicing): nk = 0, 1, …, n_s icin bollugu ≤nk\leq n_k olan turler elenir. dilimde hayatta kalan turlerle Deng entropisi yeniden hesaplanir. Boylece bolluk bilgisi dolayli olarak sisteme girer.

title: Introduction to taxdiv
text: taxdiv package provides tools calculating taxonomic diversity indices incorporate hierarchical structure biological classification. traditional diversity indices like Shannon Simpson consider species abundances, taxonomic diversity measures account evolutionary ecological relationships among species. package implements three main approaches: Classical diversity indices: Shannon Simpson Clarke & Warwick taxonomic distinctness: Delta, Delta*, AvTD, VarTD Ozkan (2018) Deng entropy-based diversity: pTO pTO+
code: library(taxdiv)

title: Introduction to taxdiv
text: use hypothetical Mediterranean forest community demonstrate package functionality. community 10 tree shrub species known abundances 4-level taxonomic hierarchy.
code: # Species abundances (individuals per plot) community <- c(   Quercus_coccifera    = 25,   Quercus_infectoria   = 18,   Pinus_brutia         = 30,   Pinus_nigra          = 12,   Juniperus_excelsa    = 8,   Juniperus_oxycedrus  = 6,   Arbutus_andrachne    = 15,   Styrax_officinalis   = 4,   Cercis_siliquastrum  = 3,   Olea_europaea        = 10 )  # Taxonomic hierarchy tax_tree <- build_tax_tree(   species = names(community),   Genus   = c("Quercus", "Quercus", "Pinus", "Pinus",               "Juniperus", "Juniperus", "Arbutus", "Styrax",               "Cercis", "Olea"),   Family  = c("Fagaceae", "Fagaceae", "Pinaceae", "Pinaceae",               "Cupressaceae", "Cupressaceae", "Ericaceae", "Styracaceae",               "Fabaceae", "Oleaceae"),   Order   = c("Fagales", "Fagales", "Pinales", "Pinales",               "Pinales", "Pinales", "Ericales", "Ericales",               "Fabales", "Lamiales") )  tax_tree #>                Species     Genus       Family    Order #> 1    Quercus_coccifera   Quercus     Fagaceae  Fagales #> 2   Quercus_infectoria   Quercus     Fagaceae  Fagales #> 3         Pinus_brutia     Pinus     Pinaceae  Pinales #> 4          Pinus_nigra     Pinus     Pinaceae  Pinales #> 5    Juniperus_excelsa Juniperus Cupressaceae  Pinales #> 6  Juniperus_oxycedrus Juniperus Cupressaceae  Pinales #> 7    Arbutus_andrachne   Arbutus    Ericaceae Ericales #> 8   Styrax_officinalis    Styrax  Styracaceae Ericales #> 9  Cercis_siliquastrum    Cercis     Fabaceae  Fabales #> 10       Olea_europaea      Olea     Oleaceae Lamiales

title: Introduction to taxdiv
text: Clarke & Warwick framework provides abundance-weighted presence/absence-based measures:
code:
# Delta: average taxonomic diversity (abundance-weighted) d <- delta(community, tax_tree) cat("Delta (taxonomic diversity):", round(d, 4), "
") #> Delta (taxonomic diversity): 2.3912  # Delta*: taxonomic distinctness (abundance-weighted, excludes same-species) ds <- delta_star(community, tax_tree) cat("Delta* (taxonomic distinctness):", round(ds, 4), "
") #> Delta* (taxonomic distinctness): 2.7668  # AvTD (Delta+): average taxonomic distinctness (presence/absence) spp <- names(community) avg_td <- avtd(spp, tax_tree) cat("AvTD (Delta+):", round(avg_td, 4), "
") #> AvTD (Delta+): 2.8667  # VarTD (Lambda+): variation in taxonomic distinctness var_td <- vartd(spp, tax_tree) cat("VarTD (Lambda+):", round(var_td, 4), "
") #> VarTD (Lambda+): 0.2489

title: Introduction to taxdiv
text: Deng entropy framework (Deng, 2016) generalizes Shannon entropy Dempster-Shafer evidence theory. taxonomic level, mass function accounts number species within group (“focal element size”), giving weight groups contain species. Ozkan (2018) uses Deng entropy construct four measures: uTO: Unweighted taxonomic diversity (uses slicing procedure) : Weighted taxonomic diversity (taxonomic levels weighted rank) uTO+: Unweighted taxonomic distance (presence/absence, nk=0 ) +: Weighted taxonomic distance Deng entropy values taxonomic level reveal contribution hierarchical level overall diversity: species level, Ed equals ln(S) = ln(10) since present species receive equal weight. higher levels, Deng correction term (2^|Fi| - 1) increases entropy groups containing species.
code:
result <- ozkan_pto(community, tax_tree)  cat("uTO  (unweighted diversity):", round(result$uTO, 4), "
") #> uTO  (unweighted diversity): 7.4895 cat("TO   (weighted diversity):", round(result$TO, 4), "
") #> TO   (weighted diversity): 10.6675 cat("uTO+ (unweighted distance):", round(result$uTO_plus, 4), "
") #> uTO+ (unweighted distance): 8.5502 cat("TO+  (weighted distance):", round(result$TO_plus, 4), "
") #> TO+  (weighted distance): 11.7283 cat("Deng entropy at each level:
") #> Deng entropy at each level: for (i in seq_along(result$Ed_levels)) {   cat("  ", names(result$Ed_levels)[i], ":",       round(result$Ed_levels[i], 4), "
") } #>    Species : 2.3026  #>    Genus : 2.5459  #>    Family : 2.5459  #>    Order : 2.9935

") #> uTO+: 8.5502 cat("TO+:", round(r1$TO_plus, 4), "

title: Introduction to taxdiv
text: deterministic pTO calculation (Run 1) uses species . sensitive result community composition? Run 2 explores randomly including excluding species 50% probability across many iterations, taking maximum pTO value observed. maximum values Run 2 always >= deterministic values Run 1, deterministic calculation included first iteration. can examine pTO values vary across iterations:
code:
set.seed(42) run2 <- ozkan_pto_resample(community, tax_tree, n_iter = 101, seed = 42)  cat("=== Run 1 (deterministic) ===
") #> === Run 1 (deterministic) === cat("uTO+:", round(run2$uTO_plus_det, 4), "
") #> uTO+: 8.5502 cat("TO+: ", round(run2$TO_plus_det, 4), "
") #> TO+:  11.7283 cat("uTO: ", round(run2$uTO_det, 4), "
") #> uTO:  7.4895 cat("TO:  ", round(run2$TO_det, 4), "

") #> TO:   10.6675  cat("=== Run 2 (max across", run2$n_iter, "iterations) ===
") #> === Run 2 (max across 101 iterations) === cat("uTO+:", round(run2$uTO_plus_max, 4), "
") #> uTO+: 8.5502 cat("TO+: ", round(run2$TO_plus_max, 4), "
") #> TO+:  11.7283 cat("uTO: ", round(run2$uTO_max, 4), "
") #> uTO:  7.4895 cat("TO:  ", round(run2$TO_max, 4), "
") #> TO:   10.6675 iter_df <- run2$iteration_results cat("uTO+ range:", round(min(iter_df$uTO_plus), 4), "to",     round(max(iter_df$uTO_plus), 4), "
") #> uTO+ range: 0 to 8.5502 cat("TO+  range:", round(min(iter_df$TO_plus), 4), "to",     round(max(iter_df$TO_plus), 4), "
") #> TO+  range: 0 to 11.7283

title: Taxonomic Diversity Indices Using Deng Entropy
text: taxdiv R package computing taxonomic diversity indices ecological community data. provides unified framework brings together classical diversity measures, Clarke & Warwick’s taxonomic distinctness family, Deng entropy-based approach Ozkan (2018) — single package. Traditional diversity indices Shannon Simpson treat species equally distinct. However, community 10 species 10 different families taxonomically diverse 10 species genus. taxdiv accounts taxonomic structure using two complementary frameworks: Clarke & Warwick (1995, 1998, 2001) — path-length-based taxonomic distinctness measures (Delta, Delta*, AvTD, VarTD) simulation-based significance testing Ozkan (2018) — Deng entropy-based taxonomic diversity (pTO) using Dempster-Shafer evidence theory slicing procedure, producing 8 complementary indices

title: Taxonomic Diversity Indices Using Deng Entropy
text: Ozkan (2018) method applies Deng entropy — generalization Shannon entropy based Dempster-Shafer evidence theory — taxonomic hierarchy data. taxonomic level k, Deng entropy Ed(k) quantifies evenly species distributed across higher-level groups (genera, families, etc.). product level-wise entropies yields taxonomic diversity (pTO), product including species-level Shannon entropy yields taxonomic distance (pTO+). slicing procedure progressively removes least abundant species, recalculating step. maximum value across slices represents community’s optimal taxonomic diversity. stochastic resampling approach (Run 2) identifies “happy” “unhappy” species — whose removal decreases increases diversity, respectively.

title: Taxonomic Diversity Indices Using Deng Entropy
text: Average Taxonomic Distinctness (AvTD/Delta+) measures average path length taxonomic tree species pairs. independent sample size, making suitable comparing studies unequal sampling effort. Variation Taxonomic Distinctness (VarTD/Lambda+) measures variance path lengths. Together simulate_td() plot_funnel(), taxdiv provides simulation-based 95% confidence funnels statistical significance testing.

what: Batch Analysis from a Single Data Frame — batch_analysis
title: Batch Analysis from a Single Data Frame — batch_analysis
text: Computes diversity indices one sample sites single data frame (e.g., imported Excel). function automatically detects site column, taxonomic columns, abundance column, splits data site, returns summary data frame species count 14 diversity indices per site.

title: Batch Analysis from a Single Data Frame — batch_analysis
code: batch_analysis(   data,   site_column = NULL,   tax_columns = NULL,   abundance_column = "Abundance",   correction = c("none", "miller_madow", "grassberger", "chao_shen"),   parallel = FALSE,   n_cores = NULL )

title: Batch Analysis from a Single Data Frame — batch_analysis
text: data data frame containing species data. Must include minimum species column, least one taxonomic rank column, abundance column. Optionally includes site/plot column multi-site analysis. site_column Character string specifying name site column. NULL (default), function searches columns named "Site", "site", "Alan", "alan", "Plot", "plot". column found, data treated single site. tax_columns Character vector specifying names taxonomic columns (Species highest rank). NULL (default), function auto-detects columns named "Species", "Genus", "Family", "Order", "Class", "Phylum", "Kingdom" (case-insensitive). abundance_column Character string specifying name abundance column. Default "Abundance" (case-insensitive match). correction Bias correction Shannon index. One "none" (default), "miller_madow", "grassberger", "chao_shen". Passed shannon(). See shannon() details. parallel Logical. TRUE, use parallel processing compute indices multiple sites concurrently. Default FALSE. n_cores Number CPU cores use parallel = TRUE. Default NULL uses parallel::detectCores() - 1.

title: Batch Analysis from a Single Data Frame — batch_analysis
text: data frame one row per site columns: Site, N_Species, Shannon, Simpson, Delta, Delta_star, AvTD, VarTD, uTO, , uTO_plus, TO_plus, uTO_max, TO_max, uTO_plus_max, TO_plus_max.

title: Batch Analysis from a Single Data Frame — batch_analysis
text: site column present (values identical), entire data set treated single community. function calculates following indices per site: Shannon: Shannon-Wiener entropy (shannon) Simpson: Gini-Simpson index (simpson) Delta: Clarke & Warwick taxonomic diversity (delta) Delta_star: Clarke & Warwick taxonomic distinctness (delta_star) AvTD: Average taxonomic distinctness (avtd) VarTD: Variation taxonomic distinctness (vartd) uTO: Unweighted taxonomic diversity (Ozkan pTO, levels) : Weighted taxonomic diversity (Ozkan pTO, levels) uTO_plus: Unweighted taxonomic distance (Ozkan pTO, levels) TO_plus: Weighted taxonomic distance (Ozkan pTO, levels) uTO_max: Unweighted taxonomic diversity (informative levels ) TO_max: Weighted taxonomic diversity (informative levels ) uTO_plus_max: Unweighted taxonomic distance (informative levels ) TO_plus_max: Weighted taxonomic distance (informative levels )

title: Taxonomic Diversity Index (Delta) — delta
text: Calculates taxonomic diversity index (Delta) Warwick & Clarke (1995). average weighted path length every pair individuals, including -species pairs (weighted 0).

title: Taxonomic Diversity Index (Delta) — delta
code: delta(community, tax_tree, weights = NULL)

title: Taxonomic Diversity Index (Delta) — delta
text: community named numeric vector species abundances. tax_tree data frame taxonomic hierarchy. weights Optional numeric vector path weights taxonomic level. NULL, linear scale used (1, 2, 3, ...).

title: Taxonomic Diversity Index (Delta) — delta
text: numeric value representing taxonomic diversity (Delta).

title: Taxonomic Diversity Index (Delta) — delta
text: $$\Delta = \frac{\sum\sum_{<j} w_{ij} x_i x_j + \sum_i 0   \cdot x_i(x_i-1)/2}{\sum\sum_{<j} x_i x_j + \sum_i   x_i(x_i-1)/2}$$

what: Calculate Deng Entropy at a Single Taxonomic Level — deng_entropy_level
title: Calculate Deng Entropy at a Single Taxonomic Level — deng_entropy_level
text: Computes Deng entropy (Ed) given set group proportions specific taxonomic level. core entropy calculation Deng (2016), generalizes Shannon entropy Dempster-Shafer evidence theory framework.

title: Calculate Deng Entropy at a Single Taxonomic Level — deng_entropy_level
code: deng_entropy_level(   abundances,   group_sizes = NULL,   correction = c("none", "miller_madow", "grassberger", "chao_shen") )

title: Calculate Deng Entropy at a Single Taxonomic Level — deng_entropy_level
text: abundances numeric vector abundances group (node) given taxonomic level. group_sizes Optional integer vector focal element sizes (|Fi|) group. species level NULL (sizes 1, reducing Shannon entropy). higher taxonomic levels, value represents number species within group. correction Bias correction method Shannon entropy estimation. applied species level (group_sizes = NULL). One "none" (default), "miller_madow", "grassberger", "chao_shen". See shannon() details. warning issued correction requested non-NULL group_sizes.

title: Calculate Deng Entropy at a Single Taxonomic Level — deng_entropy_level
text: numeric value representing Deng entropy level.

title: Calculate Deng Entropy at a Single Taxonomic Level — deng_entropy_level
text: Deng entropy calculated : $$E_d = -\sum_{} m(F_i) \ln \frac{m(F_i)}{2^{|F_i|} - 1}$$ species level, focal element cardinality 1, Deng entropy reduces Shannon entropy: $$E_d^S = H = -\sum_i p_i \ln p_i$$ higher levels (genus, family, etc.), \(|F_i|\) equals number species within group, mass function normalized proportion total abundance group. Bias correction meaningful species level Deng entropy equals Shannon entropy. higher taxonomic levels mass function different structure bias-correction formulas apply.

title: Calculate Deng Entropy at a Single Taxonomic Level — deng_entropy_level

title: Calculate Ozkan's Taxonomic Diversity Index (pTO) — ozkan_pto
text: Computes four components Deng entropy-based taxonomic diversity measure proposed Ozkan (2018): weighted/unweighted taxonomic diversity (, uTO) weighted/unweighted taxonomic distance (+, uTO+).

title: Calculate Ozkan's Taxonomic Diversity Index (pTO) — ozkan_pto
code: ozkan_pto(community, tax_tree, max_level = NULL)

title: Calculate Ozkan's Taxonomic Diversity Index (pTO) — ozkan_pto
text: community named numeric vector species abundances. Names must match first column tax_tree. tax_tree data frame taxonomic hierarchy. First column species names, subsequent columns taxonomic ranks lowest highest (e.g., Species, Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum, Kingdom). max_level Integer NULL. Maximum number taxonomic levels (Species) include product formula. NULL (default), available levels used. set "auto", function automatically detects highest informative level (Deng entropy > 0 nk=0) truncates product . positive integer limits many levels (e.g., max_level = 2 uses Genus Family).

title: Calculate Ozkan's Taxonomic Diversity Index (pTO) — ozkan_pto
text: named list components: uTO Unweighted taxonomic diversity (levels) Weighted taxonomic diversity (levels) uTO_plus Unweighted taxonomic distance (levels) TO_plus Weighted taxonomic distance (levels) uTO_max Unweighted taxonomic diversity (max informative level) TO_max Weighted taxonomic diversity (max informative level) uTO_plus_max Unweighted taxonomic distance (max informative level) TO_plus_max Weighted taxonomic distance (max informative level) Ed_levels Deng entropy taxonomic level (nk=0 slice) max_informative_level Integer: highest level Ed > 0

title: Calculate Ozkan's Taxonomic Diversity Index (pTO) — ozkan_pto
text: method uses slicing procedure Ozkan (2018). slice (nk = 0, 1, ..., n_s), species abundance <= nk removed. surviving species receive EQUAL weight (1/count) — abundance information enters indirectly species survive slice. Deng entropy taxonomic level computed using equal proportions, mass function m(Fi) = count_in_group / total_count |Fi| = number species taxonomic group. core product formula slice : $$\prod_{=1}^{L} \left( w_i \left( \frac{(e^{E_d^S})^2}   {e^{E_d^}} + 1 \right) \right)$$ \(E_d^S\) Deng entropy species level \(E_d^\) Deng entropy level , computed using presence/absence (equal weight) proportions. pTO+ (taxonomic distance) uses nk=0 slice: $$pT_O^+ = \ln \prod_{=1}^{L} \left( w_i \left(   \frac{(e^{E_d^S})^2}{e^{E_d^}} + 1 \right) \right)$$ pTO (taxonomic diversity) aggregates across slices: $$pT_O = \ln \left( \frac{\sum_{k=0}^{n_s} (n_s - n_k)   \prod_{=1}^{L} \left( w_i \left( \frac{(e^{E_d^S})^2}   {e^{E_d^}} + 1 \right) \right)}{n_s + \sum n_k} \right)$$

title: Sensitivity Analysis of Ozkan's pTO Index (Islem 3 / Run 3) — ozkan_pto_sensitivity
text: algorithm follows Excel macro's Islem 3 logic: species, inclusion probability depends jackknife classification Islem 1: Unhappy species (AA = 0, non-contributing): Included probability \((S-1)/S\), S total species count. Excel formula: (RANDBETWEEN(1, S) > 1, H2, 0). Happy species (AA > 0, contributing): Included probability derived Run 2 results. Excel formula: (L25 >= RANDBETWEEN(0, K22), H2, 0), L25 summary score Run 2 K22 iteration count. happy species probability computed : $$P_{happy} = \frac{\max(0, N_{positive} - S) + 1}{N_{iter} + 1}$$ \(N_{positive}\) number Run 2 iterations produced positive uTO+ value S species count. maximum pTO across three runs (Run 1, 2, 3) final result.

title: Shannon Diversity Index — shannon

title: Shannon Diversity Index — shannon
text: naive (MLE) Shannon index calculated : $$H' = -\sum_{=1}^{S} p_i \ln(p_i)$$ \(p_i = n_i / N\) proportion species \(\), \(N\) total number individuals, \(S\) number species observed. MLE estimator known negative bias significant small samples. Three bias-correction methods available: Miller-Madow (1955): Adds first-order bias correction term: $$H_{MM} = H_{MLE} + \frac{S_{obs} - 1}{2N}$$ Grassberger (2003): Uses digamma function instead logarithm: $$H_G = \ln(N) - \frac{1}{N} \sum_i n_i \psi(n_i)$$ \(\psi\) digamma function. Chao-Shen (2003): Applies Good-Turing coverage correction Horvitz-Thompson weighting: $$\hat{C} = 1 - f_1 / N$$ $$H_{CS} = -\sum_i \frac{\hat{p}_i \ln \hat{p}_i}{1 -   (1 - \hat{p}_i)^N}$$ \(\hat{p}_i = \hat{C} \cdot n_i / N\) \(f_1\) number singletons. Bias corrections require integer abundance counts. warning issued non-integer values detected correction != "none".

title: Shannon Diversity Index — shannon
text: Miller, G.. & Madow, W.G. (1954). maximum likelihood estimate Shannon-Wiener index diversity. AFCRC-TR-54-75. Grassberger, P. (2003). Entropy estimates insufficient samplings. arXiv:physics/0307138. Chao, . & Shen, T.-J. (2003). Nonparametric estimation Shannon's index diversity unseen species sample. Environmental Ecological Statistics, 10, 429-443.
